channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Scammers hit Christmas cheer with fake wine and festive gift emails - here's how to stay safe
item.description: Holiday-themed emails increasingly carry scams using loan forms, fake order confirmations, and credential harvesting to collect sensitive personal and financial information.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Digitalizar y cooperar para… saber y poder cuidar o curar
item.description: La transformación digital que se está produciendo en el sector salud no sigue un esquema planificado, ni responde a una estrategia explícita planteada por alguno de los múltiples actores que interactúan en nuestro sistema sanitario.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Best Boxing Day sales 2025: deals from Amazon, Currys, John Lewis, and other leading retailers today
item.description: Looking to do some post-Christmas shopping? We've laid out all of today's best Boxing Day sales in one easy to read list right here - including tech, appliances, home, and much more.
item.dc:creator: Alex Whitelock, TechRadar

item.title: How to migrate to a new Windows PC
item.description: It’s great to get a new computer, but it’s not so great to realize that all your stuff is on the old one. You’re in luck, though. It’s easier than ever to transfer your data from an old PC to a new one. We’ve covered multiple ways you can do it, below.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to watch Call the Midwife 2025 Christmas specials online
item.description: With time running out, the Nonnatus head on a rescue mission to Hong Kong. Here's how to watch the Call the Widwife 2025 Christmas specials online anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wardley, TechRadar

item.title: Virtual reality opens doors for older people to build closer connections in real life
item.description: Many retirement developments serve as a tranquil refuge for older people who can no longer travel to faraway places or engage in bold adventures.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France’s postal service was hit by a major cyberattack right before Christmas. Here are the hackers who did it
item.description: A pro-Russian hacking group claimed responsibility for a major cyberattack that halted package deliveries by France’s national postal service just days before Christmas, prosecutors said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Time to switch to eSIM? South Korea will now make consumers scan their faces to buy a SIM card and cut down on scams
item.description: South Korea now requires facial scans for SIM registration following data breaches which used fraudulently activated mobile phone numbers.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Intel shares fall on report Nvidia halts production test
item.description: Intel Corp. shares fell Wednesday after a report said that Nvidia Corp. halted a test to use Intel's production process to make advanced chips.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Waymo to begin testing robotaxis in more Bay Area cities
item.description: Driverless vehicles no longer exist solely in spy movies and futuristic dreams. In fact, Solano County streets are one step closer to a future that features these self-driving “robotaxis.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Rodriguez, The Mercury News

item.title: 3D-printed housing project for student apartments takes shape
item.description: Denmark's 3D-printed student village proves automation builds 36 apartments faster than traditional methods. Skovsporet project shows housing future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army bucks trend, to move forward with $50B MAPS contract
item.description: Despite several agencies pausing or canceling large multiple award contracts, the Army decided to proceed with its MAPS vehicle for professional services. The post Army bucks trend, to move forward with $50B MAPS contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump administration moves to overhaul how H-1B visas are granted, ending lottery system
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is replacing its lottery system for H-1B visas with a new approach prioritizing skilled, higher-paid foreign workers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New SantaStealer malware is after your passwords and crypto
item.description: New SantaStealer malware reportedly threatens holiday shoppers with password theft. This Christmas-themed info-stealer targets browsers and crypto wallets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google Cloud signs $10 billion deal with Palo Alto Networks
item.description: Google Cloud has signed a comprehensive cooperation agreement with cybersecurity company Palo Alto Networks. As part of the agreement, some of Palo Alto Networks’ current services will be moved to Google’s cloud platform. But it is also about offering new AI services to customers, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FCC bans new Chinese-made drones, citing security risks
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission says it will ban new foreign-made drones, a move that will keep new Chinese-made drones out of the U.S. market.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Data center investments break all records
item.description: New data centers are being built around the world in connection with AI initiatives. According to a compilation by S&P Global, spending will reach a record $61 billion in 2025, with the US and Canada accounting for the vast majority.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Global uncertainty is reshaping cloud strategies in Europe
item.description: When Austria’s Ministry of Economy, Energy, and Tourism set out to replace Skype for Business as its virtual meeting platform, Microsoft Teams was the obvious choice.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
